summary: I checked all 11 Wave-3 flags against the real gate-B launcher and confirmed the load-bearing facts by grep. Only ONE flag, PG_EXPERT_FACET_PLANNER, is safe to activate as-is: its new module provably fires and the old path cannot replace it (verified wired at slate:1257, preflight-required:1771, force-on:2007, allowlist:2827). Its one gap is a missing fire-marker log, which is a minor add, not a blocker. The other TEN are NOT safe. SEVEN flags — cross_source_body, numeric_comparator, provenance_reanchor, synth_primary, finding_dedup_nli, min_cite_set, two_sided_debate — are not in the gate-B slate at all (verified: zero hits in run_gate_b.py), so on a paid run they stay OFF and the OLD code builds the report with no warning. sub_entity_query_expansion is only a soft default that a stray .env=0 can silently beat (verified: only a setdefault at :3092). basket_consume_finding_dedup is wired ON but can silently do nothing and ship the old baskets with no signal. span_resolver is DEAD on the gate-B path by design — the abstractive writer pins the local-window loophole shut (verified: allow_local_window_fallback=False at abstractive_writer.py:302), so the tracer's green was wrong. Bottom line: for ten of eleven flags the wrong or old module CAN fire in place of the right one with no signal — six are P0, four are P1. overall_launch_safe is FALSE. Every activated flag must be force-wired into the slate, its hard-dependency flags protected, and a positive fire marker added, before the paid launch. Six of the P0 risks are faithfulness-SAFE (they lose coverage or a disclosure, they do not fabricate) but they still mean the module is silently absent from the paid deliverable — exactly the 'wrong module fired in place of the right one' failure the operator demands we prevent."
routing_safe: PG_EXPERT_FACET_PLANNER — verified fully wired in run_gate_b.py (slate:1257, preflight-required:1771, force-on:2007, allowlist:2827, coverage-lever assert:3234). Old path is unreachable when the flag is ON (fs_researcher_query_gen.py:493 returns _plan_expert_facet_queries before the legacy TOC loop; a degenerate LLM reply falls to the module's OWN deterministic floor, never back to legacy seeds). No silent-fallback, no unthreaded dep, both co-flags force-set + preflight-required so an operator .env=0 fails closed pre-spend. wrong_module_can_fire=false. SOLE gap is P2 observability: no R1-specific fire marker exists, so add the marker for the canary (hardening, not a launch blocker).

at_risk.risk: DARK on gate-B: verified zero occurrences in run_gate_b.py (not in slate/force-on/preflight/allowlist). On a paid run the flag defaults OFF, so cross_source_synthesis.py:722 takes the ELSE branch _anchor_candidate_pairs (OLD anchor-equality pairing) and builds every section's cross-source analytical units. Both new and old share the same '[cross_source_synthesis] composed N unit(s)' marker and the post-run gate assert_cross_source_synthesis_fired keys only off the OUTER flag PG_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS, so the run ships GREEN with zero indication the OLD pairing produced the body. Compounded by a dead barren-noop assertion (run_gate_b.py:2429 literal 'anchored' never matches the code's emitted 'candidate' at cross_source_synthesis.py:759).
at_risk.pre_launch_fix: QUAD-wire PG_CROSS_SOURCE_BODY exactly like PG_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS: add to _FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE + _BENCHMARK_FORCE_ON_FLAGS + _BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_REQUIRED_FLAGS + _WINNER_FLAG_ALLOWLIST. Emit a plan-driven-specific marker in _plan_driven_candidate_pairs and a distinct anchor-equality marker in _anchor_candidate_pairs. Thread equiv_clusters + agree_map from multi_section_generator.py:5235/5273 so plan-driven candidacy is not silently degraded to same-facet/edge/refuter only. Fix the dead barren-noop literal mismatch (run_gate_b.py:2429 vs cross_source_synthesis.py:759).

at_risk.risk: DARK on gate-B: verified zero occurrences in run_gate_b.py. Flag defaults '0' so numeric_comparator_enabled() is False -> the lookup build is skipped -> _vc_numeric_keys stays None -> cross_source_synthesis.py:650 'if rel==neutral and numeric_key_by_cluster' is falsy -> every fully-comparable pair renders the OLD neutral connective '; separately, ' on 100% of runs, no signal. Secondary: even once wired, enrichment sections structurally bypass it via the FIX-K branch (PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_RENDER_VERIFIED_SPANS=1, PG_SYNTH_PRIMARY off); and the lookup build at multi_section_generator.py:5180 swallows any exception to None with no logger. The OLD path is faithfulness-SAFE (purely additive, fails closed to neutral) so this is P0-of-INERTNESS/coverage, not P0-of-danger.
at_risk.pre_launch_fix: Add PG_NUMERIC_COMPARATOR:'1' to _FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE as a FORCE-ON member AND to _WINNER_FLAG_ALLOWLIST (otherwise the SLATE-PURITY gate at run_gate_b.py:3998 RuntimeErrors at preflight on an un-allowlisted ON-token); consider preflight-required. Add a fire-marker log at cross_source_synthesis.py:660-661 when comp is set. Change the silent swallow at multi_section_generator.py:5180 to logger.warning so an ON-flag build failure is fail-loud. Decide enrichment scope explicitly (thread numeric_key into FIX-K, or document verified-compose-primary-only).

at_risk.risk: DARK on gate-B: verified zero occurrences in run_gate_b.py and run_honest_sweep_r3.py. On a normal paid launch _provenance_reanchor_enabled() reads an unset env -> False -> the reanchor branch at provenance_generator.py:3394 is never armed -> strict_verify DROPS every wrongly-cited findings sentence via the OLD no-reanchor path on every section, with zero signal (no preflight fails closed on it, get_reanchor_telemetry() is never called, the 'reanchored:' soft_warnings tag is not in _SOFT_FLAG_MARKER_TEXT and not logged). Faithfulness-NEUTRAL (the old drop is STRICTER and every rescue re-passes the frozen verify with allow_local_window_fallback=False), so the harm is silent COVERAGE loss + an unverifiable 'activated' claim — P0 on the activation-integrity axis.
at_risk.pre_launch_fix: Wire PG_PROVENANCE_REANCHOR into run_gate_b.py like every other winner: _FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE + _BENCHMARK_FORCE_ON_FLAGS + _BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_REQUIRED_FLAGS + _WINNER_FLAG_ALLOWLIST. Wire the fire marker: call reset_reanchor_telemetry() before generation and get_reanchor_telemetry() after in run_honest_sweep_r3.py, log the snapshot. Confirm PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=enforce (:814/:2233) and PG_SPAN_RESOLVER=1 (:1064) stay force-set (they do).

at_risk.risk: DARK on gate-B (verified zero occurrences in run_gate_b.py) AND a routing/scope defect even once wired. The gate-B slate force-ON's PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE_MULTICITED (run_gate_b.py:1282, preflight-required), so verified_compose.py:2320 routes EVERY basket with >=2 distinct-origin SUPPORTS to compose_basket_multicited_sentence, which NEVER enters _compose_one_basket_synth_primary. The corroborated baskets — the entire §-1.3 consolidate-keep-all CORE report body — are composed by the OLD verbatim-K-span path (build_verified_span_draft) with no error and no log. synth-primary would reach only the tail of single-source baskets. There is also NO positive fire marker in the synth-primary path today.
at_risk.pre_launch_fix: (A) ROUTING DECISION (not just a wiring flip): if synth-primary is meant to be THE primary body writer, either route >=2-source baskets through synth-primary too, or turn PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE_MULTICITED OFF under synth-primary; if the multicited K-span path is intentionally kept for corroborated baskets, document synth-primary as explicitly single-source-only scope. (B) Add a positive fire marker inside _compose_one_basket_synth_primary authored-prose returns (verified_compose.py:1483/1486), NOT inside the fallback. (C) Wire PG_SYNTH_PRIMARY into slate + force-on + preflight-required + allowlist. (D) Add the run_gate_b post-run canary asserting the marker fires AND covers a corroborated basket.

at_risk.risk: DARK on gate-B (verified zero occurrences in run_gate_b.py; also absent from run_honest_sweep_r3.py:_PAID_PATH_WINNER_FLAGS, which gate-B never calls). Decisive gate finding_dedup.py:1479 defaults env '0' -> the directional-NLI keystone is never entered -> qualitative baskets are produced by the ALWAYS-ON legacy lexical greedy clustering (finding_dedup.py:1428-1439). Line :1479 is a bare 'if flag: run_new' with no else/assert/marker, so the old path silently stands in. Secondary (even once force-ON): silent degrade-to-legacy on a dead/OOM cross-encoder — entails_directional returns None on model-load/predict/OOM failure, edges empty, 'if not edges: return clusters' ships legacy clusters byte-identical to flag-OFF, matching the documented Wave-3 CUBLAS-OOM box.
at_risk.pre_launch_fix: Add PG_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI to run_gate_b.py in four places: slate '1', _BENCHMARK_FORCE_ON_FLAGS (hard os.environ set, NOT setdefault/floor), the preflight-required set, and the slate-purity allowlist. Also force PG_FINDING_DEDUP_QUALITATIVE='1' and PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_QUALITATIVE='1'. Add a positive keystone fire marker + degrade marker through FindingDedupResult -> manifest (finding_dedup_nli:{invoked, degraded, wall_truncated, directional_merge_count}), distinct from nli_merge_count. Confirm the basket-consumer (PG_BASKET_CONSUME_FINDING_DEDUP) reads finding_dedup clusters on the gate-B seam so a fired keystone's merges are not discarded.

at_risk.risk: DARK on gate-B (verified zero occurrences in run_gate_b.py) AND has ungated hard dependencies. Route 1 (strongest): the minimizer call is nested inside 'if _layer2_cite_enabled()' (PG_CORROBORATION_LAYER2_CITE, default '1' but ALSO absent from run_gate_b.py), and PG_CITATION_TWO_LAYER_POLICY is identically unguarded — a stray operator/.env=0 on either survives the slate (load_dotenv override=False runs BEFORE the slate) and silently no-ops the activation, rendering every member inline (OLD path). Route 2: with default PG_MIN_CITE_SET_MAX_INLINE=0 the module is prune-only on already-verified members -> returns the IDENTITY partition -> byte-identical output, and _min_cite_active is never logged/telemetried. Route 3: any exception is swallowed fail-open to the old all-inline path.
at_risk.pre_launch_fix: Add PG_MIN_CITE_SET to _FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE + _BENCHMARK_FORCE_ON_FLAGS + _BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_REQUIRED_FLAGS (force-set, fail closed pre-spend). CRITICALLY also force-on PG_CORROBORATION_LAYER2_CITE and PG_CITATION_TWO_LAYER_POLICY in the same lists (an .env=0 on either silently defeats the flag). Add a positive fire marker (log + manifest min_cite_fired:true + counts) at run_honest_sweep_r3.py:3615. Set PG_MIN_CITE_SET_MAX_INLINE>0 (or explicitly accept prune-only) so the activation has a checkable render effect; optionally record a manifest fault flag on the fail-open catch.

at_risk.risk: WIRED (verified slate:1317, floored ON, operator-flip vector closed) BUT can silently ship the OLD partition with no signal. Route 1 (P0, normal run): _regroup_graph_by_finding_dedup returns the input graph UNCHANGED at credibility_pass.py:738-739 or :854-856 whenever finding_dedup groups nothing that maps to a claim atom (e.g. an evidence_id-keying mismatch between finding_dedup member_indices and the claim-atom eid maps). The function emits NO log/telemetry on entry or merge, so _assemble_baskets builds baskets byte-identical to flag-OFF. Route 2 (P1, disclosed): a wall-timeout (3000s) or exception sets credibility_analysis=None and DISCARDS the regroup result even if it fired, shipping the legacy partition with a credibility_pass_unavailable gap. The only detector (assert_multi_origin_baskets_exist) self-skips because PG_SHALLOW_REPORT_CANARY is not in the gate-B slate.
at_risk.pre_launch_fix: Add PG_SHALLOW_REPORT_CANARY='1' to the gate-B slate so assert_multi_origin_baskets_exist and its producer telemetry line (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:19263-19265) actually run on the paid path. Add a DIRECT entry+merge log inside _regroup_graph_by_finding_dedup (credibility_pass.py:701-875) so activation is provable independent of the downstream effect and the wall-discard. Treat manifest credibility_disclosed_gap=='credibility_pass_unavailable' as a HARD pre-ship blocker. Run one small real gate-B query asserting multi_origin_baskets>=1 to prove the member_indices->annotated eid keying resolves to claim atoms.

at_risk.risk: Verified armed ONLY by os.environ.setdefault (run_gate_b.py:3092) — NOT force-on, NOT preflight-required, absent from _FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE. So any pre-existing PG_SUBENTITY_QUERY_EXPANSION=0 (operator .env via load_dotenv, a VM shell export, or a leftover default-OFF validation config) WINS silently and preflight does NOT catch it -> the flag-OFF seed-only frontier builds the corpus, GREEN, no log (the module has zero logging and the only aggregate line reports a total count that cannot distinguish sub-entity queries). Secondary (flag genuinely ON): the sub-entity slice is placed AFTER the entire baseline window, and the seed-issue loop breaks on the 5400s retrieval wall, so on any wall-pressured question the tail sub-entity queries never issue.
at_risk.pre_launch_fix: Promote from the standalone setdefault (:3092) to hard force-on: add PG_SUBENTITY_QUERY_EXPANSION to _FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE AND _BENCHMARK_FORCE_ON_FLAGS AND the preflight force-on/required set, to parity with its two mandatory co-flags PG_QGEN_FS_RESEARCHER + PG_EXPERT_FACET_PLANNER. Add a dedicated fire marker inside the widen branch (fs_researcher_query_gen.py:404-407) logging the resolved flag value + count of sub-entity queries actually issued, and emit an explicit 'sub_entity: skipped (wall passed)' disclosure. Interleave a bounded sub-entity slice WITHIN the baseline window so the 5400s wall cannot starve the new module.

at_risk.risk: DARK on gate-B (verified zero occurrences in run_gate_b.py) -> the whole module ships OFF with no signal unless the operator manually exports it. Even once wired, LEG-2 (composer disclosure) is nested inside the verified-compose elif and is silently skipped when credibility_analysis is None, when _section_baskets_for_compose returns [], or when the FIX-K enrichment branch preempts (PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_RENDER_VERIFIED_SPANS=1 + PG_ENRICHMENT_FACET_ROUTE=1 are force-on), so a facet-routed enrichment section renders on the OLD path while the debate inspection is structurally skipped. Faithfulness-SAFE: the only thing lost is an OMITTED honest asymmetry disclosure, never a fabricated con — hence P1. (LEG-1 con-guarantee is a structural no-op on gate-B because PG_EXPERT_FACET_ANGLES defaults to 5 and counter_evidence is already in _ANGLE_LENSES[:5].)
at_risk.pre_launch_fix: Force-pin PG_TWO_SIDED_DEBATE: add to _FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE + _BENCHMARK_FORCE_ON_FLAGS + preflight-required + allowlist. Add a LEG-2 fire marker inside _maybe_two_sided_debate_disclosure (~multi_section_generator.py:4974) recording section title + has_pro/has_con + whether a disclosure was appended, and a matching LEG-1 marker in expert_facet_planner._angle_lenses_for. Either run the two-sided inspection on the FIX-K/enrichment path too, or emit a 'debate-inspection-skipped route=<fixk|legacy|cred_none|no_baskets>' trace so a silently-skipped debate section is never invisible.

at_risk.risk: INERT-BY-DESIGN on the real gate-B path — the tracer's 'confirmed_reaches_new' is WRONG (it traced the legacy K-span branch). VERIFIED: PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER is slate force-on + preflight-required (run_gate_b.py:826/1694/1894), so a released gate-B run is GUARANTEED to take the abstractive body branch, whose verify_fn is make_writer_verify_fn which pins kwargs.setdefault('allow_local_window_fallback', False) (abstractive_writer.py:302, docstring P1-2). That short-circuits the NEUTRAL local-window rescue at provenance_generator.py:2544 BEFORE the re-point CAPTURE/APPLY guards (:2711/:2887), so the PG_SPAN_RESOLVER re-point fires NOWHERE on the released run — a NEUTRAL narrow span is dropped fail-CLOSED to the verbatim K-span (stricter, faithfulness-SAFE). Harm is only an invalid 'activated' claim, not a faithfulness hole.
at_risk.pre_launch_fix: Do NOT change the engine (the fallback is stricter, not weaker). CORRECT the activation claim: remove PG_SPAN_RESOLVER from any 'must fire on the paid gate-B run' set and update wave3_activate_surface_map.md + the tracer verdict — it is structurally unreachable on the abstractive contract. If the citation-precision benefit is wanted on the abstractive path, that is a NEW gated design (re-point paraphrases whose own span is NEUTRAL-but-window-entails) to be built and Codex-gated separately. Validate the existing re-point OFFLINE on the legacy/K-span path only. Add the inverted structural canary (assert the re-point marker is ABSENT on gate-B; present => routing regression => HALT).

canary_assertions.assert_new_fired_marker: A NEW positive synth-primary fire-marker (add at the authored-prose returns verified_compose.py:1483/1486, NOT inside _synth_primary_fallback_unit) appears at least once AND records the basket's distinct-origin SUPPORTS count AND fired for at least one CORROBORATED (>=2 distinct-origin SUPPORTS) basket.
canary_assertions.assert_old_not_fired: STRUCTURAL: the multicited producer (compose_basket_multicited_sentence -> build_verified_span_draft) did NOT capture 100% of >=2-source baskets — i.e. reject any run where the synth-primary marker only ever covers single-source baskets while every corroborated basket went to the OLD verbatim-K-span path. overall_rc=1 if the marker is absent OR only covers single-source baskets.

canary_assertions.assert_new_fired_marker: manifest['finding_dedup']['finding_dedup_nli'] object is PRESENT with invoked==true AND degraded==false AND wall_truncated==false (fields to be added), proving _apply_finding_dedup_nli_grouping was entered and ran to completion without the cross-encoder OOM/unavailable degrade; this keystone marker is DISTINCT from nli_merge_count (the _apply_consolidation_nli canary).
canary_assertions.assert_old_not_fired: Keystone marker absent (skipped at finding_dedup.py:1479) OR degraded==true OR wall_truncated==true => the always-on legacy lexical grouping stood in => FAIL. Do NOT assert directional_merge_count>0 (a corpus may legitimately have zero bidirectionally-entailing qualitative pairs).

canary_assertions.assert_new_fired_marker: A NEW direct entry+merge log inside _regroup_graph_by_finding_dedup (e.g. '[basket-consume] entered claims=N finding_clusters=K merged_old_to_new=M') is present; AND (with PG_SHALLOW_REPORT_CANARY enabled) the eligibility implication holds: IF finding_dedup produced >=1 multi-origin cluster THEN >=1 basket reaches composition with verified_support_origin_count>=2 (assert_multi_origin_baskets_exist).
canary_assertions.assert_old_not_fired: manifest credibility_disclosed_gap != 'credibility_pass_unavailable' (that value = the wall-timeout/exception discard that ships the legacy partition). Entry marker absent => the byte-identical no-op passthrough (credibility_pass.py:738-739/:854-856) ran => old path => FAIL.

canary_assertions.assert_new_fired_marker: os.getenv('PG_PROVENANCE_REANCHOR') resolves truthy AND os.getenv('PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT')=='enforce' (the enforce-gate hinge at provenance_generator.py:1540) AND the reanchor telemetry snapshot (reset_reanchor_telemetry before generation, get_reanchor_telemetry after — to be wired) was emitted to the run log, proving the reanchor path was reachable. Do NOT assert reanchor_recovered>0 or reanchor_attempts>0 (a fully-clean run legitimately recovers 0).
canary_assertions.assert_old_not_fired: n/a for per-sentence — the OLD drop is a legitimate fail-closed fallback whenever no candidate span passes the frozen verify. The module-dark failure (whole module off) is caught by the assert_new_fired_marker above: a falsy flag OR an absent telemetry snapshot means the old no-reanchor path ran on every section.

canary_assertions.assert_new_fired_marker: INVERTED (the re-point is inert-by-design on gate-B): assert PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER resolved truthy AND entailment mode=='enforce' (assert_activation_preconditions passed) — this PROVES the composer verify_fn is make_writer_verify_fn with allow_local_window_fallback=False, i.e. the re-point is intentionally inert on the released abstractive path. Do NOT assert the re-point 'fired' (it never fires on gate-B and would permanently false-fail).
canary_assertions.assert_old_not_fired: Assert the re-point's ONLY marker, the per-claim soft-warning literal 'reanchored_local_window:' (provenance_generator.py:2892, serialized at multi_section_generator.py:3784/4050), is ABSENT anywhere in gate-B output. If it APPEARS => HALT: the body path fell off the abstractive wrapper onto the bare verifier (a routing regression). Validate the re-point itself OFFLINE on the legacy/K-span path (allow_local_window_fallback=True), never on the paid run.

canary_assertions.assert_new_fired_marker: On a fresh (non-resume) run, a NEW R1-specific marker (add inside _plan_expert_facet_queries, e.g. '[expert_facet] R1: facets=N seed_queries=M') is present AND the issued query strings carry the facet-angle SHAPE — containing one of the fixed lens phrases ('how it works mechanism drivers' / 'who is affected stakeholders impact' / 'criticism limitations counter-evidence dissent' / 'recent trends over time change' / 'regional differences across countries').
canary_assertions.assert_old_not_fired: The legacy one-query-per-subtopic TOC query shape is ABSENT. Do NOT rely on '[fs_researcher] issued N queries' (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:10311) or '[winner-id] W2 qgen_strategy=FS-Researcher' (:12502) — neither reads PG_EXPERT_FACET_PLANNER, so both print identically whether R1 or the legacy TOC loop ran.

canary_assertions.assert_new_fired_marker: Per released question: the runtime-RESOLVED value of PG_SUBENTITY_QUERY_EXPANSION (echoed AFTER apply_full_capability_benchmark_slate, not the intended slate) == '1'; AND a NEW fire marker inside the widen branch (fs_researcher_query_gen.py:404-407) confirms widen_with_sub_entities was entered and at least one sub-entity/perspective query was ACTUALLY ISSUED into per_query_retrieve.
canary_assertions.assert_old_not_fired: NO 'sub_entity: skipped (flag off)' AND NO 'sub_entity: skipped (wall passed)' disclosure line for that question (both markers to be added). Absence of the widen fire marker => the seed-only OLD frontier built the corpus => FAIL.

canary_assertions.assert_new_fired_marker: A NEW plan-driven-specific marker inside _plan_driven_candidate_pairs / compose_cross_source_analytical_units (e.g. '[cross_source_synthesis] plan-driven pairing active') appears at least once on any run where a section reached M6 (wherever the shared '[cross_source_synthesis] composed' marker appears). Also assert PG_CROSS_SOURCE_BODY resolves truthy at spend-time.
canary_assertions.assert_old_not_fired: The OLD anchor-equality marker (add inside _anchor_candidate_pairs, e.g. '[cross_source_synthesis] anchor-equality pairing active') does NOT appear at all on the released run. Presence-of-NEW + absence-of-OLD, never a count.

canary_assertions.assert_new_fired_marker: CONFIG: os.getenv('PG_NUMERIC_COMPARATOR') truthy AND the flag is present in both the run_gate_b slate and _WINNER_FLAG_ALLOWLIST. FIRE: add a log at cross_source_synthesis.py:660-661 when comp is set (e.g. '[numeric_comparator] licensed comparison clusters A/B') and assert that EVERY rendered '; for comparison, ' connective has a matching engine-licensed log marker (i.e. every 'for comparison' is an engine-licensed upgrade, not a leaked source-verbatim phrase).
canary_assertions.assert_old_not_fired: n/a — the OLD neutral connective '; separately, ' is the correct fail-closed default for non-comparable pairs and legitimately dominates output; do NOT assert it absent. The inertness failure is detected by absence of the new fire marker, not by presence of the neutral connective.

canary_assertions.assert_new_fired_marker: _two_sided_debate_enabled()==True in the env-echo/manifest; AND a NEW LEG-2 fire marker (add in _maybe_two_sided_debate_disclosure, e.g. '[two_sided_debate] inspected section=<title> has_pro=<bool> has_con=<bool>') appears at least once on a run containing a reached debate-framed body section; AND on any one-sided debate section that composed a verified pro clause with no verified con, the disclosure prefix '[no verified counter-evidence' (multi_section_generator.py:4877) is present in that section's body.
canary_assertions.assert_old_not_fired: Branch-identity: for each debate-framed body section, the verified-compose PRIMARY log (multi_section_generator.py:5327) fired for that section AND the legacy _call_section old-path render did NOT. Absence of the LEG-2 marker on a run that contained a reached debate section => the old path silently stood in (FIX-K/enrichment collision or flag default-off) => FAIL.

canary_assertions.assert_new_fired_marker: A NEW marker at run_honest_sweep_r3.py:3615 (e.g. '[min_cite_set] active ccid=<id> inline=<k> demoted=<j>') + manifest min_cite_fired:true is present; AND all three env gates resolve truthy at spend-time: PG_MIN_CITE_SET AND PG_CORROBORATION_LAYER2_CITE AND PG_CITATION_TWO_LAYER_POLICY; AND at least one basket rendered an inline-vs-weight SPLIT (a '- SUPPORT:' bullet with NO trailing inline [N] whose evidence_id nonetheless resolves to a bibliography number) — requires PG_MIN_CITE_SET_MAX_INLINE>0.
canary_assertions.assert_old_not_fired: The fail-open fault warning at run_honest_sweep_r3.py:3618 ('[min_cite_set] wiring fault ... keeping all members inline') is ABSENT (proves no silent crash-to-old). Do NOT use an output diff (the module is byte-identical to old under default prune-only knobs).

pre_launch_fixes_ordered: 1. Force-wire the SIX dark P0 flags into run_gate_b.py as FORCE-ON (not setdefault): add each to _FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE + _BENCHMARK_FORCE_ON_FLAGS + _BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_REQUIRED_FLAGS + _WINNER_FLAG_ALLOWLIST — PG_CROSS_SOURCE_BODY, PG_NUMERIC_COMPARATOR, PG_PROVENANCE_REANCHOR, PG_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI, PG_MIN_CITE_SET, PG_SYNTH_PRIMARY. Verified: all six return zero hits in run_gate_b.py, so each is currently DARK and the OLD path builds the report with no signal. NOTE the SLATE-PURITY gate (run_gate_b.py:3998) RuntimeErrors on any ON-token flag not in the allowlist — allowlisting is mandatory, not optional.

2. Protect the ungated hard-dependency flags that silently no-op an activated flag: force-on PG_CORROBORATION_LAYER2_CITE + PG_CITATION_TWO_LAYER_POLICY (PG_MIN_CITE_SET deps — both absent from run_gate_b.py) and PG_FINDING_DEDUP_QUALITATIVE + PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_QUALITATIVE (PG_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI deps). A stray operator/.env=0 on any of these defeats the activated flag with no preflight catch.

3.
